title: Lane 3 spatial depth runway
summary: Run a parallel spatial-depth wave that strengthens front-half continuity, forest canopy/cave exploration, and high-country relief without station or route-board coupling.

execution_order.goal: Scope a front-half physical-continuity pass across beach, coastal scrub, and forest edge.
execution_order.why_now: The front half can gain spatial memory without waiting on route or station changes.

execution_order.goal: Implement the smallest front-half geometry/carrier/cue pass that improves continuity.
execution_order.why_now: Early spaces should feel authored and connected before more beta breadth.

execution_order.goal: Review front-half spatial continuity with browser proof and science-safe placement.
execution_order.why_now: Spatial density must stay readable on the handheld viewport.

execution_order.goal: Scope one forest canopy/cave loop that builds from existing vertical destinations.
execution_order.why_now: Forest is the best current foundation for magical vertical exploration.

execution_order.goal: Implement one recoverable canopy/cave loop or nook with local proof.
execution_order.why_now: A small spatial loop can make exploration feel deeper without a new traversal system.

execution_order.goal: Review canopy/cave work for readability, recovery, and no physics or cave-framework drift.
execution_order.why_now: Vertical wonder should stay cozy and controllable.

execution_order.goal: Scope one high-country relief pass for Treeline/Tundra physical memory.
execution_order.why_now: High-country traversal can deepen independently from route state and content packs.

execution_order.goal: Implement one tiny high-country relief or sheltered-descent pass with proof.
execution_order.why_now: Treeline and tundra should feel physically different, not just textually different.

execution_order.goal: Review high-country spatial work and summarize lane-3 integration risks.
execution_order.why_now: Lane 3 should end with independent proof, not a cross-lane blocker.

completion_gate: Front-half, forest vertical, and high-country spatial passes each land with local proof or are explicitly declined by scout/critic.
Browser proof is captured for any visual geometry or carrier-placement change.
No station shell, route-board logic, route catalog semantics, journal-only pack, new biome, combat, survival, or physics rewrite lands.
Focused biome/runtime tests and build pass after spatial changes.
guardrails: Lane 3 owns spatial readability and sub-ecosystem exploration.
Do not invent a cave framework or vertical HUD.
Carrier placement must stay honest to habitat and science context.

scout_475_handoff.proof_summary: Beach dune-edge and Coastal Scrub back-dune/forest-edge continuity already read cleanly at native 256x160. Forest Trail's first trailhead frame has correct entries but needs one tiny authored physical anchor before fern-hollow.
main_475_contract: Touch only src/content/biomes/forest.ts and focused forest biome tests unless proof reveals a direct local need.
Add one small trailhead log/platform around x=104-128, y=104-108, before fern-hollow and after the left corridor prompt.
Add two or three nearby authored carriers from existing forest trailhead/edge entries such as salmonberry, red-huckleberry, sword-fern, nootka-rose, or douglas-fir-sapling.
Capture fresh native 256x160 browser proof for the unchanged beach/coastal frames plus the new Forest Trail first-cover frame.
out_of_scope: Route-board logic, route catalog semantics, station shell, support choice, save schema, journal-only content packs, new route beats, new biomes, cave frameworks, vertical HUDs, and physics/traversal rewrites.
Beach dune-edge, Coastal Scrub back-dune, Coastal Scrub windbreak-swale, and Coastal Scrub forest-edge geometry unless fresh proof reveals a real regression.

runtime_changes: Added one Forest Trail trailhead log platform before fern-hollow.
Added three existing-entry trailhead carriers: salmonberry, sword-fern, and red-huckleberry.
Added focused Forest biome test coverage for the new anchor.
main_475_result.proof_summary: Fresh native 256x160 proof confirms unchanged Beach and Coastal Scrub continuity frames, the intended Forest Trail left-corridor prompt at arrival, and the new trailhead anchor around x=120 with no travel prompt in range.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/forest-biome.test.ts src/test/coastal-scrub-biome.test.ts src/test/beach-biome.test.ts src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts
local browser canvas proof at native 256x160

critic_475_review.summary: The Forest Trail trailhead anchor is local, visible at native 256x160, science-safe for the existing entries, and has no travel prompt in range at the first-cover proof frame.

scout_476_handoff.proof_summary: Existing Forest Trail cave and canopy destinations are already strong. The next step is proof-first: capture settled under-basin and upper-return frames at native 256x160, then only apply one tiny Forest-local cave-mouth or upper-return geometry/carrier adjustment if the proof shows a real readability gap.
main_476_contract: Touch only src/content/biomes/forest.ts, src/test/forest-biome.test.ts, and src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts if fresh proof exposes a real issue.
Do not edit src/engine/** unless a separately documented blocking engine bug appears; game.ts, climb-navigation.ts, and vertical-cues.ts are read-only context for this step.
Start with browser proof for under-basin-settled, upper-return-window, and optional branch-nursery-context frames under output/lane-3-main-476-forest-canopy-cave-loop-proof/.
If current geometry already reads as a settled recoverable nook, close main-476 as proof-only with no geometry change.
If implementation is needed, prefer a tiny reposition or width tweak to an existing cave-mouth/upper-return platform or one existing-entry carrier placement inside the x=372-424, y=76-108 band.
out_of_scope: Station shell, route-board logic, route catalog semantics, Source to Shore route state, journal-only content packs, close-look/sketchbook additions, content-roster expansion, new biomes, new cave systems, new climbables, vertical HUDs, and physics/traversal rewrites.
Another upper-canopy shelf or another lower cave chamber.

main_476_result.proof_summary: Fresh native 256x160 browser proof confirms the existing Forest Trail under-basin, cave-trunk cue, upper-return window, and filtered-return mouth already read as one recoverable physical route. No geometry, carrier, traversal, station, route-board, journal, or physics change was needed.
verification: local browser canvas proof at native 256x160

critic_476_review.summary: The Forest canopy/cave loop proof is sufficient and no runtime change was needed. Native 256x160 proof confirms the under-basin, cave-trunk cue, upper-return window, and filtered-return mouth route is readable and recoverable without new geometry, new climbables, framework drift, or station/route-board scope.
verification_reviewed: Implementation report verification: npm test -- --run src/test/forest-biome.test.ts src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts

scout_477_handoff.proof_summary: Treeline's Source Shelter edge remains separated after the earlier fix and Tundra's meltwater thread is readable, but Tundra's snow-meadow drift rest at x=252 still shares range with the HIGH COUNTRY MAP prompt. The next step is one Tundra-local drift-rest separation without moving the map post.
main_477_contract: Touch only src/content/biomes/tundra.ts, src/test/tundra-biome.test.ts, and optionally src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts.
Try a tiny rightward nudge of snow-meadow-drift-rest from x=252 into roughly x=266-270, adjusting only the paired snow-meadow-drift-sedge and snow-meadow-drift-ptarmigan carriers as needed.
Keep the pocket inside snow-meadow, preserve a readable gap before thaw-skirt-entry-heave at x=306, and add no new platform, carrier, route beat, prompt, or content.
Do not edit src/content/world-map.ts, map-return posts, travel framing, station, route state, save schema, physics, traversal helpers, or journal-only content.
Capture fresh native 256x160 proof under output/lane-3-main-477-high-country-relief-proof/ showing the drift rest settled with nearbyTravelTarget null and the later meltwater thread still clean.
out_of_scope: New high-country pockets, new climbables, cave/vertical frameworks, route-board or station changes, new species/science copy, map-return relocation, and player physics changes.

runtime_changes: Moved Tundra snow-meadow-drift-rest from x=252 to x=268.
Kept the two existing drift carriers on the same pocket offsets at x=274 and x=290.
Updated focused Tundra biome expectations for the local nudge and the remaining thaw-skirt gap.
main_477_result.proof_summary: Fresh native 256x160 browser proof confirms the shifted snow-meadow drift rest now settles with nearbyTravelTarget null while the later meltwater bank/thread frames remain clean.
verification: local browser canvas proof at native 256x160

critic_477_review.summary: The Tundra snow-meadow drift-rest nudge is local, proof-backed, and lane-safe. Native 256x160 proof confirms the shifted drift rest and later meltwater frames have nearbyTravelTarget null, with no map-post relocation, new route beat, station work, content expansion, save change, physics change, or traversal-framework drift.
verification_reviewed: Implementation report verification: npm test -- --run src/test/tundra-biome.test.ts; npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "Tundra|tundra|meltwater|thaw"; npm run build

post_packet_path: If clean, lane 3 can propose a later expedition-grade vertical chapter.
If a route or station need appears, write a handoff for lane 1 or lane 4.
